title: A Chemical Engineer Inspired Heuristic Optimization Algorithm Based on Reaction–Feedback–Cascade Mechanism
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper proposes a novel heuristic optimization algorithm, inspired by the thinking and working mechanisms of chemical engineers in chemical process optimization. The algorithm transforms the reaction, separation, and flow optimization of chemical processes into mathematical optimization steps and introduces a unique Reaction–Feedback–Cascade Cycle (RFCC) mechanism. By simulating the control strategies used by chemical engineers in experiments and plant operations, the algorithm achieves an organic combination of local exploration and global search, and introduces secondary objective feedback to adjust candidate solutions, improving optimization efficiency. This paper presents a complete mathematical model, dynamic control formulas, and iterative update strategies, providing a new heuristic solution method for complex continuous optimization problems.
steps_raw: This paper proposes a novel heuristic optimization algorithm, inspired by the thinking and working mechanisms of chemical engineers in chemical process optimization. The algorithm transforms the reaction, separation, and flow optimization of chemical processes into mathematical optimization steps and introduces a unique Reaction–Feedback–Cascade Cycle (RFCC) mechanism. By simulating the control strategies used by chemical engineers in experiments and plant operations, the algorithm achieves an organic combination of local exploration and global search, and introduces secondary objective feedback to adjust candidate solutions, improving optimization efficiency. This paper presents a complete mathematical model, dynamic control formulas, and iterative update strategies, providing a new heuristic solution method for complex continuous optimization problems.
